73314.name: Settler Intelligence Briefing
73314.description:
Hostile rogue drones hunt and scavenge every system in this constellation we call Phoenix. They grow stronger as you move away from Angelice. They are reported more commonly near planets but sightings occur almost anywhere.

Large asteroid clusters have been cataloged in each system. In some, a large rogue drone structure appears to be devouring the raw ore and so the Settlers have begun calling the clusters 'Moon Eater'. Another location with dense resources has been found, but exploration attempts have failed due to an infested Abaddon who sits on watch. Ophidia Operations, based in Serpentis, are working on a technology to provide concealment.

Your starter pack contains a blueprint we believe will provide access to the system Askur, which is currently locked by sophisticated encryption. We don't know what Askur has in store.

Settler security forces have not yet been established, guard yourself. However, should your ship be destroyed, starter packs can be purchased from the market here in Angelice.

77518.name: Lens 3X
77518.description: For use with Crude Extractors only.

77818.name: Unstable Fuel
77818.description: Long abandoned and left to the mercy of the Static Shear, little energy or even structure remains. This lingering dross provides barely enough of a spark to power a vessel. Even the best refinery processes cannot prevent the decay of time from claiming their product.

78504.name: Tempo CD43
78504.description: Engine with a balance of performance and power requirements. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

82425.name: LAI
82425.description: An ultra-lightweight combat vessel designed to focus on striking fast. (placeholder)

83525.name: Purified Moon Cutting Laser
83525.description: The violence necessary to obtain such a trophy has reduced its functionality to a fraction of its potential. Yet, this laser remains operational in the face of all the Frontier’s hostilities, natural and computational alike. Fitting a feralized component, even one with its protocols exorcised and rebuilt, can be considered an act of hubris.

84556.name: Smart Turret
84556.description: A structure with a turret slot which you can equip with any turret.

84955.name: Heavy Gate
84955.description: Establish your own stargate by connecting two long distance Smart Gates together.

87120.name: Heavy Printer
87120.description: Industrial printer that handles materials in large quantities.

87160.name: Refuge
87160.description: Offers a respite after a long journey. Offers fitting services.

87161.name: Field Refinery
87161.description: Cheap and portable refinery that goes anywhere with you.

87162.name: Field Printer
87162.description: Portable printer that can produce basic survival items.

87566.name: Field Storage
87566.description: A small storage unit to store your resources.

88063.name: Refinery
88063.description: A refinery capable of extracting main minerals from all ores.

88064.name: Heavy Refinery
88064.description: Industrial refinery capable of extracting most of the minerals from all ores.

88067.name: Printer
88067.description: A printer that handles materials in bulk.

88068.name: Assembler
88068.description: Assembles materials into end products like modules.

88069.name: Mini Berth
88069.description: Small shipyard for building shuttles and corvettes.

88070.name: Berth
88070.description: A shipyard for building frigates, destroyers and cruisers.

88071.name: Heavy Berth
88071.description: A large shipyard for building battleships and battlecruisers.

88086.name: Mini Gate
88086.description: Establish your own stargate by connecting two short range Small Gates together.

88092.name: Network Node
88092.description:
Establish your base with a Network Node. Stabilizes and fuels other structures in its range. Must be placed at an unclaimed Anchor point.

Once built you can set it as a home base.

88093.name: Shelter
88093.description: A hangar for ship sizes up to and including frigates.

88094.name: Heavy Shelter
88094.description: A large hangar for ship sizes up to and including battlecruisers.

88100.name: Wall 1
88100.description: A large structure used to create a physical obstacle for defensive purposes.

88101.name: Wall 2
88101.description: A large structure used to create a physical obstacle for defensive purposes.

89775.name: SEER I
89775.description: Sate the hunger for the arcane. This monolith resonates with long-forgotten knowledge.

89776.name: SEER II
89776.description: Sate the hunger for the arcane. This monolith resonates with long-forgotten knowledge.

89777.name: HARBINGER I
89777.description: Gnaw on the bones of the lost world. Monuments like this one have once sat at the eyes of the Feral storms.

89778.name: HARBINGER II
89778.description: Gnaw on the bones of the lost world. Monuments like this one have once sat at the eyes of the Feral storms.

89779.name: RAINMAKER II
89779.description: Follow the slipstream towards the source of world's blood. This sculpture is imprinted with Frontier's primal forces.

89780.name: RAINMAKER I
89780.description: Follow the slipstream towards the source of world's blood. This sculpture is imprinted with Frontier's primal forces.

89794.name: Defiled Crude Matter
89794.description:
Human greed carves gashes into the surface. Past sins bind to the structures within, transforming the promised bounty into a twisted relic. It enters this universe broken and scarred, carrying only a fraction of its potential.

Yet it is still Crude. It can still rouse dreams thought long extinct. Perhaps even the accursed dream responsible for its creation.

90184.name: Relay
90184.description: Establishes a connection to EVE Net, allowing the purchase of data, thus enabling the fabrication Crude mining lenses, as well as the sale of feral data.

91205.name: Redundant Module
91205.description: A fragment of endless growth, stripped of purpose yet stubbornly persistent.

91206.name: Void Residue
91206.description: A lingering trace of chaotic growth, the remnants of energy that once fueled a self-propagating anomaly.

91209.name: Singularity Reactor Fragment
91209.description: Small but potent, it pulses with the force that drives endless growth.

91210.name: Void fragments
91210.description: Shards born from the collapse of unstable matter, whispering of relentless expansion and unseen forces.

91374.name: Char
91374.description: Charred remnants of volcanic crust, these fire-scarred asteroids contain crystalline feldspar minerals forged in the intense heat of differentiated parent bodies. Their burnt, weathered surfaces hide valuable aluminum and silicon-rich crystals beneath layers of space-weathered rock. Char asteroids are a common source of Feldspar Crystals and can typically be found in Inner Belts.

91375.name: Slag
91375.description: Industrial waste from the cosmos—metallic slag asteroids are the leftover cores of shattered worlds. Rich in platinum and palladium, these slowly-cooled metallic matrices represent the most precious remnants of planetary formation, concentrated in dense nickel-rich grains. Slag asteroids are a common source of Platinum-Palladium Matrix and can typically be found in Inner Belts.

91376.name: Ingot
91376.description: Pure metallic ingots drifting through space, these asteroids are nature's own refined metal bars. Containing the densest materials known—iridium, osmium, and ruthenium—they're the treasure vaults of the asteroid belt, forged in the deepest, hottest cores of primordial worlds. Ingot asteroids are a common source of Iridosmine Nodules.

91377.name: Comet
91377.description: Primitive water-bearers from stellar formation, these comet-like asteroids hold preserved secrets in their hydrated sulfide cores. Part ice, part metal, they're time capsules of aqueous chemistry now frozen in the void. Comet asteroids are a common source of Hydrated Sulfide Matrix and can typically be found in Outer Belts.

91378.name: Dewdrop
91378.description: Delicate spheres of frozen volatiles, dewdrop asteroids glisten with crystalline methane ice preserved at near-absolute-zero temperatures. These fragile outer belt wanderers are reservoirs of pure cryogenic fuel, waiting to be harvested from the cold reaches of space. Dewdrop asteroids are a common source of Methane Ice Shards.

91379.name: Ember
91379.description: Still glowing with primordial organic heat, ember asteroids contain pristine kerogen—complex carbon polymers that have remained unaltered since formation. These dark, coal-like bodies hold valuable chemical building blocks. Ember asteroids are a common source of Primitive Kerogen Matrix.

91380.name: Glint
91380.description: Shimmering with rainbow-like iridescence, glint asteroids sparkle with veins of complex aromatic hydrocarbons. Radiation-forged through extended exposure, their surfaces gleam with polycyclic organic compounds—the cosmic equivalent of liquid crystal displays, beautiful and chemically exotic. Glint asteroids are a common source of Aromatic Hydrocarbon Veins.

91381.name: Soot
91381.description: Dark as coal dust and deeply primordial, soot asteroids are coated in prebiotic tholin nodules—tar-like organic compounds formed through radiation chemistry. These sooty spheres are laboratories of cosmic chemistry, their surfaces slowly processing in the radiation of deep space. Soot asteroids are a common source of Tholin Nodules.

91701.name: Printer
91701.description: A printer that handles materials in bulk.

91702.name: Heavy Printer
91702.description: Industrial printer that handles materials in large quantities.

91703.name: Refinery
91703.description: A refinery capable of extracting main minerals from all ores.

91704.name: Heavy Refinery
91704.description: Industrial refinery capable of extracting most of the minerals from all ores.

91705.name: Mini Berth
91705.description: Small shipyard for building shuttles and corvettes.

91706.name: Berth
91706.description: A shipyard for building frigates, destroyers and cruisers.

91707.name: Heavy Berth
91707.description: A large shipyard for building battleships and battlecruisers.

91708.name: Assembler
91708.description: Assembles materials into end products like modules.

91709.name: Shelter
91709.description: A hangar for ship sizes up to and including frigates.

91710.name: Heavy Shelter
91710.description: A large hangar for ship sizes up to and including battlecruisers.

91711.name: Mini Gate
91711.description: Establish your own stargate by connecting two short range Small Gates together.

91712.name: Heavy Gate
91712.description: Establish your own stargate by connecting two long distance Smart Gates together.

91717.description: Establishes a connection to EVE Net, allowing the purchase of data, thus enabling the fabrication Crude mining lenses, as well as the sale of feral data.

91720.name: Wall 1
91720.description: A large structure used to create a physical obstacle for defensive purposes.

91721.name: Wall 2
91721.description: A large structure used to create a physical obstacle for defensive purposes.

91722.name: RAINMAKER I
91722.description: Follow the slipstream towards the source of world's blood. This sculpture is imprinted with Frontier's primal forces.

91723.name: RAINMAKER II
91723.description: Follow the slipstream towards the source of world's blood. This sculpture is imprinted with Frontier's primal forces.

91724.name: HARBINGER I
91724.description: Gnaw on the bones of the lost world. Monuments like this one have once sat at the eyes of the Feral storms.

91725.name: HARBINGER II
91725.description: Gnaw on the bones of the lost world. Monuments like this one have once sat at the eyes of the Feral storms.

91726.name: SEER I
91726.description: Sate the hunger for the arcane. This monolith resonates with long-forgotten knowledge.

91727.name: SEER II
91727.description: Sate the hunger for the arcane. This monolith resonates with long-forgotten knowledge.

91751.name: Refuge
91751.description: Offers a respite after a long journey. Offers fitting services.

91752.name: Field Refinery
91752.description: Cheap and portable refinery that goes anywhere with you.

91753.name: Field Printer
91753.description: Portable printer that can produce basic survival items.

91756.name: Field Storage
91756.description: A small storage unit to store your resources.

91871.name: Nest
91871.description: A facility for storing Shells and transferring between them.

91937.name: Calamitous Marrow
91937.description: Chaos permeates the Frontier at all scales, converting stabilizing forces into agents of transformation. The evolutionary havoc pulsing through the trabeculae of this substance tantalizes with the possibilities it may unlock. Its desirable qualities, in turn, ensure its rarity, for the conditions which lead to its creation are impossible to replicate.

91978.name: Nursery
91978.description: A facility for growing synthetic Shells.

91997.name: Lens 9001X
91997.description: For use with Crude Extractors only.

92165.name: Nursery
92165.description: A facility for growing synthetic Shells.

92166.name: Nest
92166.description: A facility for storing Shells an transferring between them.

92279.name: Mini Turret
92279.description: A small base defence turret. Especially effective against smaller targets.

92280.name: Mini Turret
92280.description: A small base defence turret. Especially effective against smaller targets.

92389.name: Skip
92389.description:
A transit module for covering ground. Once engaged, the vessel holds its heading and is carried forward until the burn completes. An ancient choice for hopping between adjacent points of interest.

Among the oldest schemata found in the Frontier. It is simulated that countless variants have descended from a theorised original pattern, each adapted to different hulls and purposes, yet the core principle remains unchanged. The first makers understood something fundamental about motion that later work has only decorated. ( copy )

92394.name: Fine Young Crude Matter
92394.description:
Tight weaves of energetic currents tangle and dissipate beneath the surface. Deeper, they merge so seamlessly as to appear singular in nature, concealing the true chaos seething within. The hue surrounding the matter subtly reflects this hidden truth, oscillating wildly between wave forms.

It is chaos which offers potential, enticing all who might stumble upon its power. So tempting is the possibility that risks may be easily ignored.

92401.name: Turret
92401.description: A medium base defence turret. Especially effective against mid sized ships.

92404.name: Heavy Turret
92404.description: A large base defence turret. Especially effective against larger ships.

92406.name: Turret
92406.description: A medium base defence turret. Especially effective against mid sized ships.

92407.name: Heavy Turret
92407.description: A large base defence turret. Especially effective against larger ships.

92414.name: Fine Old Crude Matter
92414.description:
Dense braids of tranquil, potent energy dance across the surface. Within, they ply together as if seeking to become one. From this unmet desire, the fuel of dreams might be extracted, as practiced by those long passed.

The ethereal beauty of transience emanates from compacting, ever-shifting weaves of sinew. Appreciation goes beyond immediate need and can therefore be discarded.

92421.name: Leap
92421.description:
A transit module for covering ground. Once engaged, the vessel holds its heading and is carried forward until the burn completes. An ancient choice for hopping between adjacent points of interest.

Among the oldest schemata found in the Frontier. It is simulated that countless variants have descended from a theorised original pattern, each adapted to different hulls and purposes, yet the core principle remains unchanged. The first makers understood something fundamental about motion that later work has only decorated. ( copy ) ( copy )

93141.name: Field Cairn
93141.description:
A Field Cairn is deployable anchor that fixes a signature to an exact point in space for those intending to return.

Cairns lack stabilizing thrusters and are inherently unstable, gradually degrading and drifting out of orbit within 24 hours.

93228.name: Field Cairn
93228.description:
A Field Cairn is deployable anchor that fixes a signature to an exact point in space for those intending to return.

Cairns lack stabilizing thrusters and are inherently unstable, gradually degrading and drifting out of orbit within 24 hours.
